title: File Storage System
prompt: Design a cloud file storage system like Google Drive or Dropbox. Users should be able to upload and download files, share files with others, sync files across devices, and the system should handle deduplication to save storage.

functional: Share files and folders with other users (view/edit permissions)
Sync files across multiple devices in near real-time
Content-based deduplication to reduce storage costs
nonFunctional: Upload/download latency proportional to file size, not system load
Consistency: all devices eventually see the same state within seconds
Support resumable uploads for large files

keyQuestions: What is the maximum file size we need to support?
Do we need real-time collaboration or just sync?
What sharing models are required (link sharing, user-based)?
Do we need file versioning? How many versions to keep?
What platforms need sync support (desktop, mobile, web)?

keyCalculations: Storage: 500M users * 2GB avg = 1 EB raw, with dedup ~500 PB
Metadata DB: 500M users * 200 files avg * 1KB = 100 TB metadata
Bandwidth: 10K uploads * 5MB avg = 50 GB/sec upload bandwidth

expectedEndpoints: POST /api/v1/files/upload/init { fileName, fileSize, parentId } => { uploadId, chunkUrls[] }

expectedEntities: users: id, email, name, storage_used, storage_limit, created_at
files: id, owner_id, parent_folder_id, name, size, content_hash, is_folder, created_at, updated_at
file_versions: id, file_id, version_number, content_hash, size, created_at, created_by
shares: id, file_id, shared_with_user_id, permission (view/edit), created_at
sync_events: id, user_id, file_id, event_type, timestamp, device_id

expectedComponents: Block Storage Service (S3-like object store for chunks)
Dedup Service (content-addressable storage with hash lookup)
Sync Service (long-polling or WebSocket for device sync)
Metadata Database (SQL for file tree, permissions)
Message Queue (upload processing, sync event fan-out)

suggestedTopics: Chunking strategy: fixed-size vs content-defined chunking (Rabin fingerprint) for dedup efficiency
Sync protocol: conflict resolution when two devices edit the same file offline
Deduplication: per-user vs global, hash collision handling, security implications of cross-user dedup
Resumable uploads: how to handle partial chunk uploads and network failures
Consistency model: how to ensure all devices converge to the same state
Storage tiering: hot/warm/cold storage for cost optimization

anchors.1: Skipped requirements, jumped straight to architecture without understanding scope
anchors.2: Asked about file size limits but missed sync model or sharing requirements
anchors.3: Covered upload/download, sharing, sync, and estimated storage needs
anchors.4: Proactively identified dedup scope, conflict resolution needs, platform constraints, and storage cost implications

anchors.1: Monolithic service storing files directly in database with no chunking
anchors.2: Separated file storage from metadata but no chunking, sync, or dedup
anchors.3: Chunked uploads, object storage, metadata DB, sync service with clear data flow
anchors.4: Content-addressable storage with dedup, resumable uploads, efficient sync protocol, and clear separation of read/write paths

anchors.1: No depth on any component, hand-waved chunking and sync
anchors.2: Basic discussion of chunking but no conflict resolution or dedup details
anchors.3: Deep dive into chunking + dedup or sync with concrete algorithms and trade-offs
anchors.4: Thorough analysis of content-defined chunking, vector clocks for sync, cross-user dedup security, and storage cost modeling

checklist: Strong vs eventual consistency for sync

anchors.1: Could not identify any trade-offs in the design
anchors.2: Mentioned one trade-off (e.g., SQL vs NoSQL) without deeper analysis
anchors.3: Discussed 2-3 trade-offs with clear pros/cons and justified decisions
anchors.4: Systematically evaluated trade-offs across storage, consistency, and cost with quantitative reasoning

commonMistakes: Storing entire files as single objects instead of chunking for large file support
Not addressing sync conflicts when the same file is edited on multiple devices offline
Ignoring security implications of cross-user deduplication (side-channel attacks)
Using polling instead of push-based sync, leading to high QPS and latency
Not designing resumable uploads for large files over unreliable networks
Treating metadata and file content with the same storage strategy
